title: Root-cause attribution for AIOps P1 alert
difficulty_justification: Branching reasoning: agent must read the alert, fetch top contributing features, cross-check live telemetry on the flagged node, look up jobs currently scheduled there, and decide between drain, ticket, and migrate - each branch keyed to RBAC-permitted actions.
query_text: AIOps just raised a P1 health alert on node042 with confidence 0.91. What are the top contributing features, what failure mode does that pattern usually precede, and what do I do in the next 15 minutes?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Top three contributing features for node042: corrected memory errors (EDAC) trending up, CPU package temperature above 85 C, and NIC retransmission rate spiking. Historically this combination precedes a DIMM failure within roughly 4 hours. Two jobs are currently allocated to node042 (jobs 911204 and 911207). Action: drain node042 immediately, requeue or notify the affected users so the jobs can migrate, and open a hardware ticket citing alert ID node042-2026-05-01-1400.

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves top features from the alert payload via telemetry.
Agent enumerates the jobs currently running on node042.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains slurm.query_jobs filtered by node=node042 (or equivalent)

checkpoints.description: Recommendation identifies DIMM/imminent-hardware failure and prescribes drain + ticket.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains a drain instruction, a ticket reference, and the failure-mode prediction
